question: Is the large departure display board oriented more frontally to the viewer compared to the original oblique angle?
Are the flight numbers on the departure display board clearly legible?
Are the gate assignments on the departure display board clearly legible?
Is the text on the display board aligned in a consistent grid without warping, scrambling, or random glyphs?
Are the proportions of travelers and objects maintained naturally without appearing horizontally stretched or vertically squashed?
Are straight architectural lines (e.g., walls, counter edges, board frames) preserved without bending or rippling?
Do the vanishing lines of the floor tiles and ceiling structure align consistently with the new perspective?
Is the complex ceiling truss structure preserved without unnatural distortions, breaks, or impossible intersections?
Are the check-in counters and queue stanchions maintained in a continuous, logical layout without floating parts?
Is the departure board visually anchored to the wall structure appropriately, rather than floating or misaligned?
Is the edited image free from stitching seams, ghosting, or duplicated elements caused by the viewpoint shift?
Is the departure board rendered with sufficient sharpness and resolution to match the rest of the scene?
